macro_summary: AI infrastructure spending remains the dominant market theme heading into a dense earnings week, with TSMC reporting Wednesday July 16 as the first major bellwether for AI capex health. The Fed's July 29 FOMC decision coincides with Microsoft earnings, creating a binary macro/micro event on the same day. Semis have broadly de-rated despite consensus EPS growth of +131% for Q2, suggesting valuation has reset to levels not seen since 2023. Options structures are broadly preferred over outright stock exposure given elevated implied volatility and macro headline risk.
screening_note: 6 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. All 5 qualifying ideas cleared that bar, with 3 appearing in all 6 runs.
key_events: TSM Q2 2026 earnings — Wednesday July 16 (this week, 3 days away)
SKHY first full permanent-ticker trading day on Nasdaq — TODAY July 13
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings + FY2027 CapEx guidance — July 29 after close (FOMC same day)
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings + Tim Cook final earnings call — July 30 after close
SKHY new share issuance enters KOSPI index — July 29 (supply event to monitor)

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 6 overnight screens with 5 high-conviction votes, making it the top consensus idea. The stock is ~30% off its all-time high and trades at ~19x FY2027 forward earnings — its cheapest multiple since 2023 and below the S&P 500's ~21.5x — despite an AI ARR run-rate above $37B growing 123% YoY. The July 29 earnings print will be the first release of FY2027 CapEx guidance, a pivotal data point for AI monetization credibility, and coincides with the FOMC decision — making options with defined risk the preferred structure.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings + first FY2027 CapEx guidance release — July 29, 2026 after market close (FOMC also July 29)
ideas.edge: Consensus analyst price target ~$589-592 implies ~56% upside from current ~$382; AI ARR >$37B growing 123% YoY; HSBC projects Q4 revenue $89.3B (+16.8% YoY) on 39.6% Azure growth; decade-cheapest forward multiple
ideas.bull_case: Azure reaccelerates to 39%+ growth and FY2027 CapEx guidance signals sustained AI monetization, triggering a multiple re-rating from 19x toward the historical 25-28x range; consensus $589 target becomes achievable within 12 months.
ideas.bear_case: Azure growth disappoints below 35% or FY2027 CapEx comes in light, signaling AI demand softness; hawkish FOMC on the same day adds rate pressure on long-duration tech multiples, compounding any earnings miss.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 runs, though with mostly medium conviction (5/6), reflecting excitement tempered by post-IPO risk. Today is the first full regular-session trading day under the permanent SKHY ticker after the record $26.5B Nasdaq IPO on July 10 ($149 price, opened at $170, 7x oversubscribed). The structural thesis rests on SK Hynix's dominant HBM position supplying NVDA and AAPL, the CEO's own guidance for a 'worst-ever' memory shortage in 2027, and a 'Korea Discount' re-rating as the Nasdaq listing structurally closes a historical 35% valuation gap vs. Micron.
ideas.catalyst: First full permanent-ticker trading day TODAY July 13; new share issuance enters KOSPI July 29 (near-term supply event); options market expected to develop within days/weeks
ideas.edge: IPO 7x oversubscribed; Korean shares up 250%+ in 2026; historical 35% Korea Discount vs. Micron expected to compress on Nasdaq listing; CEO guided 'worst-ever' HBM shortage past 2030; 2025 earnings +117% YoY
ideas.bull_case: Korea Discount compression plays out as US institutional investors gain direct access, HBM supply tightness sustains pricing power through 2027, and a pullback toward $155-160 offers a cleaner entry with strong risk/reward.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO pop has already priced in significant re-rating; KOSPI new issuance on July 29 creates supply overhang; options liquidity remains thin on a 3-day-old US listing; chasing at $168+ risks buying a peak.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 5 of 6 runs with 4 high-conviction votes. The stock has de-rated ~17% from its May peak and now trades at ~21.7x forward earnings — the same multiple as the S&P 500 — despite 85% YoY revenue growth, $75.2B data center revenue last quarter, and ~70-80% AI GPU market share. Goldman Sachs notes this compares to a 5-year average forward P/E of 72x. A cluster of hyperscaler earnings (TSMC July 16, MSFT July 29, GOOGL/META/AMZN in July) provide near-term binary re-rating catalysts, making a defined-risk call spread the preferred structure.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC earnings July 16 (direct demand proxy); hyperscaler AI CapEx guidance throughout July (MSFT July 29, GOOGL, META, AMZN); Q2 semis EPS consensus +131% YoY
ideas.edge: Forward P/E 21.7x vs. 5-year average ~72x (Goldman Sachs); stock ~17% off May peak despite 85% revenue growth; HBM supply sold out through most of 2027; BofA top pick; hyperscaler capex projected to top $1T in 2027 vs. $650B this year
ideas.bull_case: TSMC beats and raises July 16 and hyperscalers confirm $1T+ AI capex trajectory in July reports, triggering a multiple re-expansion from 21x back toward 35-40x on undiminished growth — call spreads offer asymmetric upside.
ideas.bear_case: Any hyperscaler capex guidance disappointment or pause in AI spending directly impairs the demand outlook; geopolitical Taiwan risk or US export control escalation remains a persistent tail risk on the stock.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 4 of 6 runs with balanced conviction. A confirmed July 30 earnings date follows a quarter where iPhone sales grew 22% and total revenue hit $111.2B (+17% YoY). The company guided 14-17% revenue growth for the June quarter, EPS consensus sits at $1.88 (+19.8% YoY), and an active $100B buyback provides a floor. Near-term narrative is amplified by Tim Cook's final earnings call and a rumored fall foldable iPhone lineup, while being only ~4% behind NVDA in market cap — a strong print could trigger index and momentum flows as it vies for world's most valuable company.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after market close; Tim Cook final earnings call (CEO transition); foldable iPhone debut rumored for fall 2026
ideas.edge: Company-guided 14-17% revenue growth for June quarter; EPS consensus $1.88 (+19.8% YoY); 8 consecutive EPS beats; Services $31B/quarter at 32% margins; consensus analyst target ~$315 vs. ~$284 current (~11% upside); $100B buyback active
ideas.bull_case: Strong June quarter print with raised full-year guidance and foldable iPhone confirmation triggers momentum and index flows; Services margin expansion reinforces premium multiple; Tim Cook transition event drives media attention and retail buying.
ideas.bear_case: 37x earnings multiple is stretched in a hawkish rate environment; recent pullback to $284 suggests some institutional distribution; any iPhone demand softness in China or tariff-related margin commentary could reset expectations sharply.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
ideas.thesis: TSM appeared in 3 of 6 runs with all 3 being high-conviction votes — the cleanest conviction ratio of any idea in today's digest. Earnings are this Wednesday July 16, just 3 days away, with Citi placing it on a 30-day upside catalyst watch. The company guided Q2 revenue of $39-40.2B (~32% YoY growth) and Q1 gross margins already reached 66.2%. A beat-and-raise would serve as a direct positive read-through for NVDA, AMD, and AAPL, potentially catalyzing a broad AI semis complex recovery. The stock has pulled back ~9% in the past week, creating an entry ahead of a binary event.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings report Wednesday July 16, 2026; Citi 30-day upside catalyst watch active; consensus expects full-year guidance raise
ideas.edge: Q2 EPS expected $3.80 vs. $2.47 YoY (+54%); Q2 revenue expected ~$40B (+33% YoY); average analyst target ~$520 implies ~17-20% upside from ~$433; trades ~22x NTM vs. peers at 40x+; ~9% pullback in past week; global chip sales +93.9% YoY per SIA
ideas.bull_case: Beat-and-raise on July 16 with full-year guidance lifted above 30% growth confirms AI capex is intact, re-rates TSM toward peer multiples and acts as a rising tide for the entire AI semis supply chain including NVDA and AAPL.
ideas.bear_case: Taiwan geopolitical risk is a persistent and unhedgeable tail; any soft demand signals from Apple or AMD orders would disappoint a market pricing in perfection; a guidance miss would cascade negatively across the AI semis complex.
